tagline: Parshuram Kund, the Golden Pagoda, and the Lohit River
description: The easternmost accessible districts of Arunachal Pradesh. Lohit has Parshuram Kund — a Hindu pilgrimage site on the Lohit River where a January bathing fair draws lakhs of pilgrims. Namsai has the Golden Pagoda — a striking Theravada Buddhist temple built by the Khamti people.

destinations.name: Parshuram Kund
destinations.tagline: A Hindu kund where the Lohit cuts through a gorge
destinations.description: Parshuram Kund is where the Lohit River cuts through a narrow gorge and a natural bathing pool forms. Hindu pilgrimage site — according to legend, Parshuram washed away the sin of killing his mother here. January Makar Sankranti mela draws 50,000+ pilgrims. The site itself, outside the festival, is a peaceful gorge.
what_is_there: The Kund itself — natural pool in a gorge
January Mela — one of the largest religious fairs in Northeast India
local_tips: January Mela: arrive a day early, accommodation books out completely
Outside January the site is peaceful and nearly empty
Tezu town (35km) is the base — no accommodation at the Kund

destinations.name: Golden Pagoda (Kongmu Kham)
destinations.tagline: A Theravada Buddhist temple unlike anything else in India
destinations.description: The Golden Pagoda in Namsai is a Theravada Buddhist monastery complex built by the Khamti people — a Thai-related ethnic group unique to this corner of Arunachal. The architecture is distinctly Southeast Asian — more Myanmar or Thailand than Tibet. Often called the most beautiful monastery in Northeast India.
what_is_there: Golden Pagoda — the main temple, gold-covered, reflecting pool
Monks in residence — Theravada tradition, different from the Tibetan Buddhism of Tawang
Khamti village nearby — see traditional weaving
Evening light is the best time — the gold catches the sunset
local_food: Khamti cuisine — Thai-influenced, rice-based, distinctly different from rest of Arunachal
Fish dishes — Khamti are river people
local_tips: Visit in the early morning or evening — midday light is harsh for photos
Remove footwear before entering the temple
The Khamti weaving is among the most intricate in Arunachal — buy directly from weavers

from_guwahati.notes: 2 days from Guwahati. Dibrugarh is the practical base for this region. Namsai is 50km from Tinsukia — doable as a day trip from Assam.

incitetales_angle: The Golden Pagoda in Namsai is Theravada Buddhist — more Myanmar or Thailand than Tibet. One of the most beautiful monastery complexes in Northeast India.
